title: Cleartext Industrial Credentials in PCAP-Derived Metadata

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=ot_sec (sourcetype="claroty:alert" OR sourcetype="nozomi:alerts") earliest=-7d
| where match(_raw,"(?i)password=|passwd=|user=.*pass") AND NOT match(_raw,"(?i)\\*\\*\\*")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<proto>modbus|opcua|dnp3).*"
| stats count by proto, src_ip, dest_ip, signature
| sort - count
description: Scans IDS metadata for username or password fields in cleartext industrial protocols where confidentiality must be preserved.
value: Scans IDS metadata for username or password fields in cleartext industrial protocols where confidentiality must be preserved.

visualization: Table, Single value (hits), Bar by protocol.

grandmaExplanation: Scans metadata for username or password fields in cleartext industrial protocols where confidentiality must be preserved — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Claroty xDome TA, Nozomi TA.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `claroty:alert` or `nozomi:packets_meta` (example sourcetype).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Never index full payload in prod—use IDS summaries; (2) rotate exposed creds; (3) enable TLS where vendor supports; (4) segment sniffers.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_sec (sourcetype="claroty:alert" OR sourcetype="nozomi:alerts") earliest=-7d
| where match(_raw,"(?i)password=|passwd=|user=.*pass") AND NOT match(_raw,"(?i)\\*\\*\\*")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<proto>modbus|opcua|dnp3).*"
| stats count by proto, src_ip, dest_ip, signature
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cleartext Industrial Credentials in PCAP-Derived Metadata (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 4.1)** — Scans IDS metadata for username or password fields in cleartext industrial protocols where confidentiality must be preserved.

Documented **Data sources**: `claroty:alert` or `nozomi:packets_meta` (example sourcetype). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Claroty xDome TA, Nozomi TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_sec; **sourcetype**: claroty:alert, nozomi:alerts. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_sec, sourcetype="claroty:alert", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(_raw,"(?i)password=|passwd=|user=.*pass") AND NOT match(_raw,"(?i)\\*\\*\\*")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Extracts fields with `rex` (regular expression).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by proto, src_ip, dest_ip, signature** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.src, IDS_Attacks.dest, IDS_Attacks.signature | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Cleartext Industrial Credentials in PCAP-Derived Metadata (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 4.1)** — Scans IDS metadata for username or password fields in cleartext industrial protocols where confidentiality must be preserved.

Documented **Data sources**: `claroty:alert` or `nozomi:packets_meta` (example sourcetype). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Claroty xDome TA, Nozomi TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value (hits), Bar by protocol.
